goals.g1: Does detect_overlaps() correctly implement Compound Match Resolution's step 1 (find all overlapping match regions), covering both the overlapping_nodes case and the boundary_of_one_inside_other case, without authoring a new graph2 (per decision_schema_reuse)?

c1.statement: detect_overlaps reuses _read_boundary_externals (existing, unmodified) to read each match's live boundary-crossing targets against the SAME base_graph, read-only, no teardown -- consistent with decision_schema_reuse

c2.statement: region_of[n] accumulates every match index whose OWN binding.values() includes real node n -- the framework's overlapping_nodes case when len>=2

c3.statement: boundary_crossings[(i,j)] is populated only when node n is in match i's boundary_touches AND in match j's region_of AND i != j -- exactly the framework's boundary_of_one_inside_other definition

c4.statement: hand-built test 1 (B bound to A's own-region node r2) produced overlapping_nodes={r2:{0,1}}, boundary_crossings={} -- correct classification

c5.statement: hand-built test 2 (B bound to A's live crossing target r3, outside A's region) produced overlapping_nodes={}, boundary_crossings={(0,1):{r3}} -- correct classification

c6.statement: degree counts distinct match indices touching a node via region OR boundary, matching the framework's rank-by-overlap-degree requirement

c7.statement: nodes touched by only one match were correctly excluded from both dicts and got degree 1 in both tests

c8.statement: detect_overlaps correctly implements Compound Match Resolution step 1 for both the overlapping_nodes and boundary_of_one_inside_other cases, reusing existing per-rule schema machinery with no new graph2 authored.

derived_from.rule: two independent hand-built scenarios each isolate one classification branch and both matched the framework's definitions with no cross-contamination

ans.statement: detect_overlaps correctly implements Compound Match Resolution step 1 for both the overlapping_nodes and boundary_of_one_inside_other cases, reusing existing per-rule schema machinery with no new graph2 authored.

k1.method: constructed and ran two distinct hand-built scenarios exercising each classification branch separately and inspected the returned dicts directly
k1.result: test1: overlapping_nodes={r2:{0,1}}, boundary_crossings={}. test2: overlapping_nodes={}, boundary_crossings={(0,1):{r3}}. Both match the framework's definitions exactly

committed.statement: detect_overlaps correctly implements Compound Match Resolution step 1 for both the overlapping_nodes and boundary_of_one_inside_other cases, reusing existing per-rule schema machinery with no new graph2 authored.
